summary:
The term “bobble head doll syndrome” refers to a rare condition linked to third ventricular dilation, which is characteristic of hydrocephalus. It results from disruptions in cerebrospinal fluid circulation and absorption—key features of hydrocephalopathy—which establishes a direct pathological connection between the two conditions despite its informal name. 

Final Answer: Yes.

The summary should be concise and strictly based only on what was stated by Agent2; do not add external knowledge or interpretation beyond their provided text.

• Third ventriculardilation = part of hydrocephalus pathology 
• Hydrocephalus disrupts CSF flow → causes enlarged ventricles

Second confirms underlying neurology matches known hydrocephalus mechanisms.
